cancer_history_summary:
Age: 72
Sex: Female
Cancer type: Glioblastoma, IDH-wildtype (left temporal lobar origin)
Histology: Diffuse astrocytic glioma, WHO Grade 4; marked cellularity, brisk mitoses (>15/HPF), pseudopalisading necrosis, microvascular proliferation, Ki‑67≈38%; GFAP+, OLIG2+, Nestin+; EMA–, Synaptophysin–
Current extent: Widely infiltrative progressive disease involving left temporo‐parieto‑insula region and new right posterior parietal white matter, diffusely non‑enhancing T2/FLAIR abnormality with increasing volume, substantial vasogenic edema producing sulcal effacement and early ventriculomegaly suggesting raised intracranial pressure; ECOG ≥ 4, prognosis measured in weeks‑months
Biomarkers:
- IDH‑wildtype (negative IDH1 R132H IHC, no pathogenic IDH1/2 mutations)
- EGFR focal amplification (persistent)
- PDGFRA amplification (~8 copies)
- CDK4 amplification (~6 copies)
- NF1 truncating LOF (p.Arg251*)
- MTAP homozygous deletion
- TERT promoter mutation c.-124C>T
- MGMT promoter unmethylated
- Low tumour mutational burden (~4 Mut/Mb)
- Microsatellite stable
- PTEN intact
- ATRX retained, TP53 wild‑type pattern
- IHC profile: GFAP+, OLIG2+, Nestin+
Treatment history:
# 02/2020: Maximal safe stereotactic/craniotomy resection of left temporal GBM (gross‑subtotal/debulk)
# 02/2020‑08/2020: Concurrent temozolomide 75 mg/m² PO daily + EBRT 60 Gy/30 fractions
# 09/2020‑03/2021: Adjuvant temozolomide 150 mg/m² days 1‑5 q28 days (≥6 cycles)
# 11/2021‑01/2022: Bevacizumab 10 mg/kg iv q2 wk started; held after 3 doses due to grade 3 proteinuria, restarted Jan 2022
# 01/2022‑04/2022: Continuous bevacizumab 10 mg/kg iv q2 wk → partial radiologic response (~20% shrinkage)
# 05/2022‑06/2022: Brief bevacizumab pause; resumed two additional cycles with further modest regression
# 07/2022: Second craniotomy/re‑resection of left temporal recurrence (EGFR amplified)
# 11/2022‑Early 2023: Continued bevacizumab maintenance; slow radiologic progression
# 02/2025: Third stereotactic‑guided craniotomy with gross‑total resection of recurrent left temporal lesion
# 03/2025‑04/2025: Temozolomide rechallenge (150‑200 mg/m² d1‑5 q28 d) post‑steroid tapers
# 04/2025‑08/2025: Compassionate‑use Phase I/II trial—oral PDGFRα TK inhibitor + metronomic temozolomide 50 mg/m² po daily; SD ~12 mo; toxicities ‑ grade 2 rash, transient lymphopenia, trace proteinuria
# 08/2025‑04/2026: Trial off‑label; reinstituted bevacizumab 10 mg/kg iv q2 wk monotherapy; initial stabilization then new right‑posterior parietal non‑enhancing infiltrate signifying progression
# 05/03/2026: Discontinued all anticancer therapy (bevacizumab withdrawal) and formally enrolled in hospice care; maintained dexamethasone 4 mg po qid, levetiracetam 1000 mg bid, antihypertensives (atenolol 50 mg + amlodipine 5 mg daily) for symptom control
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: Chronic uncontrolled hypertension historically worsened by VEGF blockade, presently managed with dual therapy but BP remains mildly elevated (~150/90 mmHg). Persistent trace proteinuria attributed to cumulative anti‑angiogenic exposure. Ongoing high‑dose dexamethasone leading to potential glucose intolerance and immunosuppression. Moderately depressed myelosuppression (WBC 3.2×10⁹/L, ANC 1.2×10⁹/L) while platelets adequate. Liver function tests within normal limits (AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, Alk Phos 85 U/L, total bili 0.7 mg/dL). Renal function preserved (eGFR > 80 mL/min/1.73 m²). ECOG ≥ 4 meets exclusion criteria for curative/intensity oncologic trials.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 52  
Sex: Female  
Cancer type: Breast cancer  
Histology: Invasive ductal carcinoma, Nottingham grade II  
Current extent: Mixed response – partial regression of hepatic metastases, stable/healing L4 vertebral metastasis; overall disease assessed as partial response per RECIST v1.1  
Biomarkers: ER 80 %+, PR 70 %+, HER2‑negative (IHC 0, FISH non‑amplicated); Ki‑67 ≈ 22 %; PIK3CA H1047R activating; ESR1 D538G mutation; TP53 R175H loss‑of‑function; CDK4 amplification; FGFR1 amplification; GATA3 R361C variant; microsatellite stable; tumour mutational burden 4 mut/Mb; BRCA1/BRCA2 wild‑type; Serum CA 27‑29 38 U/mL (declining)  
Treatment history:  
# 2020‑07‑07: Core needle biopsy confirms ER⁺/PR⁺/HER2‑ invasive ductal carcinoma.  
# 2021‑01‑19: Comprehensive NGS identifies listed somatic alterations.  
# 2021‑06‑23 to 2023‑09‑08: Letrazole daily + Ribociclib 600 mg oral Days 1‑21 /q28 d – partial radiographic response; ribociclib halted Sep 2023 for grade 2 hepatotoxicity and permanently stopped Feb 2024.  
# 2024‑02‑26 to 2024‑08‑03: Letrazole continued as sole endocrine therapy.  
# 2024‑08‑04 to 2025‑04‑xx: Fulvestrant 500 mg IM q28 d + Everolimus started 10 mg PO daily, reduced to 5 mg PO daily after month 12 owing to grade 2 stomatitis and hyperglycaemia; everolimus discontinued May 2025.  
# 2025‑05‑02 present: Fulvestrant 500 mg IM q28 d monotherapy.  
# 2025‑05‑XX: Stereotactic body radiotherapy to solitary L4 vertebral metastasis (total 20‑30 Gy in 5‑10 fractions) – achieves durable local control.  
# 2025‑12‑YY to Present: Phase II trial enrollment – Alpelisib 300 mg PO daily + Fulvestrant 500 mg IM q28 d; ongoing with best response partial response/stable disease (latest MRI/PET‑CT June 2026).  
# 2025‑12‑YY to Present: Zoledronic acid 4 mg IV q4 weeks with pre‑medication (acetaminophen 650 mg PO, Diphenhydramine 25 mg PO) – continued for bone metastasis prevention.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: ECOG 1; no untreated or evolving CNS metastases; cardiac function satisfactory; renal function normal (Creatinine 0.84‑0.85 mg/dL, eGFR ≥ 90 mL/min); mild grade 1‑2 transaminitis (AST/ALT ≤ 2× ULN) considered tolerable; hyperglycaemia secondary to alpelisib, currently managed with metformin 1000 mg BID (plans for escalation/intensification); prior grade 1 infusion reaction to zoledronic acid effectively pre‑mediated; no active HBV, HCV, HIV, nor interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis; no contraindicating cardiovascular, respiratory, hepatic, or hematologic disorders.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 68
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Small‑cell lung carcinoma (extensive‑stage)
Histology: Classic small‑cell carcinoma, WHO grade 8041/3; diffusely synaptophysin+, moderately CD56+, focally chromogranin A+, Ki‑67 ≈85%, TTF‑1 –, PD‑L1 ≤1%
Current extent: Progressive metastatic disease – newly identified hypermetabolic hepatic lesion in segment VII (1.6 cm, SUVmax 7.8) confirming disease progression; remaining sites (right lower‑lobar lung mass ∼4.1 cm, mediastinal nodes, left adrenal nodule 3.0 cm, right femoral lytic lesion, treated lumbar spine lesion) stable; no new osseous or central nervous system disease.
Biomarkers: TP53 missense c.470G>T (p.V157F) VAF 38%; RB1 homozygous deletion exons 9‑20; MYC amplification (~11 copies); STK11 missense c.1060T>C (p.F354L) VAF 27%; CREBBP missense c.4862G>A (p.R1621H) VAF 31% (VOUS); absent activating EGFR, KRAS, NRAS, HRAS alterations; no ALK, ROS1, RET, NTRK fusions; PD‑L1 ≤1%. Tumor markers: baseline NSE 18 ng/mL (stable); LDH risen to 480 U/L (reference ≤280). Recent chemistry reveals mild transaminitis (AST 48 U/L, ALT 52 U/L, alk phos 115 U/L).
Treatment history:
# Jan 2023 → Dec 2023: Carboplatin AUC 5 + Etopsidie 100 mg/m² days 1‑3 q21d (4 cycles) + single‑fraction 8 Gy palliative RT to L3 after Cycle 1 ⇒ Partial response (~30 % shrinkage).
# Jan 2024: Prophylactic cranial irradiation 20 Gy in 5 fractions ⇒ No intracranial relapse.
# Jun 2024 → Jun 2025: Amrubicin 35 mg/m² days 1‑3 q21d (4 cycles) ⇒ Stable disease; toxicities included Grade 3 thrombocytopenia (Cycle 1), Grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy (Cycle 3), Grade 3 neutropenia leading to dose reduction (after Cycle 5).
# Sep 2025 → Present (Oct 2025): Lurbinectedin 3.2 mg/m² iv Day 1 q21d (dose reduced to 2.8 mg/m² after Grade 3 neutropenia post‑Cycle 5) ⇒ Modest adrenal regression, extracranial stability until October PET‑CT demonstrated new hepatic metastasis = progressive disease. Continued supportive measures (zoledronic acid q12wks, opioid/acetaminophen-codeine, gabapentin, filgrastim as indicated).
Planned forth‑line option discussed: temozolomide oral 200 mg/m² Days 1‑5 q28d (not yet commenced).
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
• No untreated/uncontrolled brain metastases; recent MRI negative.
• ECOG Performance Status 1‑2.
• Persisting myelosuppression: ANC 900 cells/µL, hemoglobin 9.2 g/dL requiring oral iron, platelets 112 x10⁹/L.
• Mild hepatic dysfunction attributed to hepatic metastasis (transaminases modestly elevated).
• Peripheral neuropathy Grade 2 stable; chronic back pain controlled with NSAIDs.
• Comorbidities: hypertension (lis​nopril), hyperlipidemia (atorvastatin), osteoporosis (Ca/Vit D supplements).
• No active infections, autoimmune pneumonitis, or severe cardiopulmonary compromise.
Thus, presently eligible for several phase I/II studies contingent upon laboratory recovery, with no overt exclusion criteria beyond observed cytopenias and mild liver enzyme elevations.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 21  
Sex: Male  
Cancer type: High‑grade conventional osteosarcoma (primary distal femur) – metastatic to lung  
Histology: Conventional high‑grade osteogenic sarcoma, FNCLCC Grade III; SATB2+, vimentin+, desmin patchy, CK AE1/AE3−, S‑100−, p63/p40−, Ki‑67 variable (≈45 %).  
Current extent: Isolated right lower‑lobe pulmonary metastasis, now 1.6 × 1.2 cm (incremental radiographic progression from 1.4 cm), no additional visceral or skeletal disease – oligoprogressive disease.  
Biomarkers: 
- IHC: SATB2 strong nuclear, vimentin diffuse, desmin patchy, CK AE1/AE3 negative, S‑100 negative, p63/p40 negative.  
- Comprehensive NGS (523‑gene panel on metastatic lung specimen): TPTP53 p.R175H pathogenic (VAF 38 %), PTEN p.R233* likely pathogenic (VAF 27 %), RB1 loss (loss of heterozygosity), MDM2 amplification ≈6‑fold, CDK4 amplification ≈5‑fold, ERBB2 (HER2) amplification ≈8‑fold, KRAS p.G12D VUS (VAF 7 %), NF1 frameshift VUS (VAF 5 %), ATRX duplication VUS (VAF 4 %). Tumor mutational burden low (3.2 mut/Mb), MSI-stable. PD‑L1 IHC pending/not reported.  
Treatment history:  
# Nov 2020–Jan 2021: Neoadjuvant MAP (high‑dose methotrexate ×5, doxorubicin 75 mg/m² q3 wk ×2, cisplatin 120 mg/m² q3 wk ×2); tolerable.  
# Jun 2021: Wide excision of distal femur with endoprosthetic reconstruction; R0 margins, >85 % necrosis.  
# Jul 2021–Dec 2021: Adjuvant MAP (identical regimen, 6 cycles); completed uneventfully.  
# Apr 2022: Detection of solitary right lower‑lobe pulmonary nodule (~1.2 cm); observation.  
# Feb–Mar 2023: Core needle biopsy confirmed metastasis; salvage ifosfamide 2 g/m² d1‑5 + etoposide 100 mg/m² d1‑5 q21 d ×2 cycles; disease progressed (to 2.0 cm).  
# Sep 2023–Present: Off‑label pembrolizumab 200 mg IV q3 weeks; induced partial response down to 1.4 cm, then stabilized (SD) through 2024. Developed Grade 2 immune‑mediated colitis (responsive to prednisone tapers), now resolved.  
# Late 2024 (age 21): Consolidative stereotactic body radiation therapy (SBRT) to right lower‑lobe lesion, total dose 30 Gy in 5 fractions; acute toxicity limited to Grade 1 dermatologic erythema, now resolved.  
# Ongoing (2024): Continuation of pembrolizumab 200 mg IV q3 weeks as maintenance; supportive care for fatigue and emerging dry cough (guaifenesin, inhaled budesonide/formoterol PRN).
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No untreated or uncontrolled brain metastases, no cardiovascular compromise, no interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis, normal renal and hepatic function, no active viral hepatitis or HIV. Prior immune‑mediated colitis completely resolved; current mild leukopenia (WBC 3.8 ×10^9/L, ANC 1.9 ×10^9/L) stable and does not preclude proposed therapies. No other prohibited comorbidities.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 64
Sex: Male
Cancer type: HER2‑positive gastro‑oesophageal junction adenocarcinoma
Histology: Poorly differentiated signet‑ring cell adenocarcinoma
Current extent: Progressive metastatic disease with dominant hepatic lesion now 4.6 cm (growth from 4.1 cm), increasing peritoneal carcinomatosis with moderate‑volume ascites, enlarging retroperitoneal/paraaortic lymph nodes (max 1.8 cm), stable esophageal/junction wall thickening (~5 cm), prior irradiated L3 vertebral lesion quiescent, overall classified as progressive disease
Biomarkers: HER2 (ERBB2) amplification confirmed by IHC 3+ and ISH (ratio ≥ 2.2, copies ≈ 9); Microsatellite instability stable (MSI‑S); Tumor mutation burden low (~5 mut/Mb); KRAS G12D missense; TP53 R175H missense; CDH1 truncating (E738*); PIK3CA H1047R missense; MET amplification (copies ≈ 6); FGFR2 amplification (copies ≈ 4); ARID1A loss of heterozygosity; SMAD4 R361C missense; PD‑L1 combined positivity score ≈ 15 (reported); Serial CEA rise to 18 ng/mL (baseline <5 ng/mL)
Treatment history:
# 09/2020‑02/2021: CAPOX (capecitabine 1000 mg/m² bid d1‑14 q21 d)+Trastuzumab (loading 8 mg/kg iv then 6 mg/kg q3 wks); best response stable disease, stopped for rising CEA
# 04/2021‑10/2021: FOLFIRI (irinotecan 180 mg/m², levofolinate 400 mg/m², bolus 5‑FU 400 mg/m², continuous 5‑FU 2400 mg/m² over 46 h) q14 d x 8 cycles; initial stable disease then progression
# 05/2023: Laparoscopic gastro‑jejunostomy (palliation of outlet obstruction); procedure only
# 01/2024‑01/2024: External beam radiation to L3–L4 vertebral bodies, total 30 Gy in 10 fx; partial radiographic response, no neurotoxicity
# 07/2024‑present*: Pembrolizumab 200 mg IV q3 wks (initiated as third‑line immunotherapy targeting PD‑L1 CPS 15); attained stable disease at 12 weeks, subsequently progressive disease evidenced clinically and radiographically
# 07/2024‑present*: Capecitabine (Xeloda) 1000 mg/m² orally twice daily Days 1‑14 q21 d as maintenance concomitant with pembrolizumab (intermittent adherence due to nausea); ongoing at time of last encounter
(*dates approximated from documentation)
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No uncontrolled central nervous system disease; hepatic synthetic function marginally reduced (Albumin 3.2 g/dL) but meets eligibility thresholds; creatinine normal; cardiac function satisfactory; grade 2 immune‑mediated hypothyroidism controlled on levothyroxine; grade 3 anemia addressed with transfusion and planned ESA support; no active infectious hepatitis or HIV; no prohibitive autoimmune disorders; performance status ECOG 1.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 70
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Prostate adenocarcinoma
Histology: Adenocarcinoma, Gleason 9 (pattern 4 + 5), Grade Group 5
Current extent: Metastatic castration‑ resistant prostate cancer, bone‑predominant, showing biochemical progression (PSA increase to 3.0 ng/mL) and probable new osseous lesion in left femur (clinical pain); mixed metabolic response on PSMA PET/CT with residual disease in T10, left ischium, new left scapular focus, and suspected femoral involvement; ECOG 1
Biomarkers: 
- Immunohistochemistry: PSA strong+, NKX3.1 nuclear+, AMACR cytoplasmic+
- FISH: TMPRSS2‑ERG fusion‑positive
- Somatic/germline DNA repair panel: Pathogenic BRCA2 nonsense, CDK12 nonsense, CHEK2 frameshift, PTEN homozygous deletion, RB1 loss, TP53 p.R248W missense, AR amplification (~CN 7), SPOP missense (likely pathogenic); VUS: FOXA1 missense, ATM missense; MSI‑stable, TMB low (4.2 muts/Mb)
- Ki‑67 ≈45%
Treatment history:
# 2020‑06 – 2020‑09: Robotic‑assisted radical prostatectomy with extended pelvic lymph node dissection; pathology pT3a N1 (3/14 nodes positive), positive surgical margins, Gleason 9
# 2020‑10‑20 present: Leuprolide 22.5 mg IM q3 mo (continuous ADT)
# 2021‑03‑13 to 2021‑04‑28: Abiraterone acetate 1000 mg PO daily + Prednisone 5 mg BID
# 2021‑04‑28 to 2022‑11‑18: Enzalutamide 160 mg PO daily
# 2022‑11‑19 to 2023‑10‑23: Apalutamide 240 mg PO daily (with ADT)
# 2023‑10‑24 to mid 2025: Rucaparib 600 mg PO BID + Low‑dose Enzalutamide 80 mg PO daily (investigational phase II trial) concurrent with ADT
# Early 2025 (≈3 wk pause): Temporary discontinuation of Rucaparib owing to Grade 3 thrombocytopenia; platelets recovered ≥150 x10⁹/L
# Mid 2025 to early 2026: Restarted Rucaparib 600 mg PO BID + Enzalutamide 80 mg PO daily; weekly CBC monitoring instituted
# 2026‑04‑30 to present: Reduced‑dose Rucaparib 400 mg PO BID + Enzalutamide 80 mg PO daily (maintenance dose after prior hematologic toxicity) with continued ADT
# 2023‑10‑24 to present: Zoledronic acid 4 mg IV q12 weeks (initiated pending renal clearance confirmation); calcium 1000 mg + vitamin D₃ 800 IU daily supplementary
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: Controlled hypertension on amlodipide 5 mg daily; no untreated/uncontrolled central nervous system metastases; no significant cardiomyopathy, hepatic insufficiency, or renal dysfunction (creatinine ∼0.93 mg/dL, eGFR ≥ 78 mL/min); HBV/HIV/HCV negative/not reported; remote grade 2 seizure‑like event resolved; prior Grade 3 thrombocytopenia resolved; performance status ECOG 1; no other standard trial exclusion criteria identified.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 76
Sex: Female
Cancer type: High‑grade serous ovarian carcinoma
Histology: High‑grade serous carcinoma
Current extent: Metastatic disease with stable hepatic metastases and new subcentimetric peritoneal implants indicative of early progression
Biomarkers:
- Germline/pathogenic somatic BRCA2 truncating mutation (c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22)
- TPTP53 likely pathogenic missense variant (c.743G>A, p.Arg248Gln)
- Bi‑allelic ATM loss (whole‑gene deletion)
- MYC high‑level amplification (~5‑fold)
- Homologous recombination repair deficient (HRD‑score 78, HRD‑positive)
- Tumor mutational burden 4.2 mutations/Mb (low)
- microsatellite stable
- Immunohistochemistry: PAX8+, WT‑1+, CK7 strong diffuse, p53-null pattern, Ki‑67 ≈70%, ER focally weak+, PR−, HER2 0‑1+

Treatment history:
# 2021‑02‑08 (approx.): Optimal cytoreductive surgery – total abdominal hysterectomy, bilateral salpingo‑oophorectomy, infracolic omentectomy, peritoneal biopsies; confirmed stage IVB high‑grade serous carcinoma.
# 2021‑2022 (≈6 cycles): Carboplatin AUC 5 + Paclitaxel 175 mg/m² IV q21 d; best response near‑complete remission of bulk disease and partial hepatic response (RECIST PR).
# 2022‑05‑xx onward: Niraparib maintenance 300 mg PO daily, reduced to 200 mg after Grade 3 thrombocytopenia; continued ~12 months with disease stabilization; discontinued owing to recurrent thrombocytopenia.
# 2025‑08‑xx: Trabectedin 1.5 mg/m² IV day 1 q21 d; completed 2 cycles; mixed response – hepatic lesions grew (from 2.2 cm to 3.5 cm) and a new 1.0 cm lesion appeared; therapy held.
# 2025‑10‑xx: Commenced Rucaparib 600 mg PO BID (post‑Trabectedin progression); ongoing as of latest review; provides stable hepatic disease although peritoneal micromets now evident.
# 2026‑01‑xx (estimated): Image‑guid­ed radiofrequency ablation of dominant hepatic metastasis (pre‑treated lesion formerly ~4.2 cm); post‑procedure imaging shows necrotic center, stable size.
# 2026‑02‑xx (estimated): Stereotactic body radiation therapy to solitary para‑aortic lymph node, 30 Gy delivered in 5 fractions; resultant fibrosis with no viable disease.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of common trial exclusion criteria (no untreated brain metastases, no uncontrolled cardiovascular or respiratory disease, normal renal and hepatic reserve, no active infectious hepatitis/HIV, no recent severe pancytopenia, no autoimmune pneumonitis).

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 46
Sex: Male
Cancer type: T-cell lymphoblastic lymphoma
Histology: Precursor/high-grade T-cell lymphoblastic lymphoma (TdT⁺, CD3⁺, CD7⁺, CD1a⁺, Ki‑67 ≈ 90%)
Current extent: Progressive metastatic disease involving mediastinum (persistent SUVmax 12, size ~3 cm), bilateral pulmonary nodules increasing to up to 1.2 cm, and three hepatic lesions (largest 2.1 cm) consistent with metastatic deposits; associated hepatic infiltrative injury causing grade 3 transaminitis; overall ECOG 2, notable cachexia.
Biomarkers:
- Immunohistochemistry/surface: CD3+, CD7+, CD1a+, TdT+, Ki‑67 ≈ 90%; CD20-, CD19-, CD22-, ALK-, MPO-, EBER-
- Genomics (archival tumor sequencing): NOTCH1 p.V1578F activating mutation (VAF ~ 38%); JAK3 p.V678M gain-of-function mutation (VAF ~ 27%); PTEN homozygous deletion; TAL1-ST5 in-frame fusion (tumor fraction ~ 15%); microsatellite stable, low tumor mutational burden
- Virology/Hepatitis background: Resolved HBV exposure (HBsAb+, anti-HBc IgG+, HBsAg‑, HBV DNA undetectable); HAV IgG positive; HCV RNA negative
- Laboratory trends reflective of disease activity: persistently elevated LDH (~420 U/L), grade 3 elevations in AST (112 U/L) and ALT (138 U/L), ALP ↑ (210 U/L), hemoglobin mildly lowered (11.2 g/dL), neutropenia (ANC 1.0 ×10^3/µL)
Treatment history:
# 07/2020 → 02/2022: Front-line Hyper-CVAD alternating with high-dose MTX/Cytarabine (four cycles); best response partial reduction of mediastinal bulk (from 9 cm to ≈ 4 cm).
# Late 2022 → Mid 2023: Nelarabine salvaged two cycles; resulted in disease stability without further bulk decrease.
# 10/2024 → 11/2024: Palliative external-beam radiotherapy to anterior mediastinum, total dose 30 Gy in 10 fractions; produced marked local shrinkage to ≈ 3 cm and symptomatic relief.
# 12/2024 → 01/2025: Azacitidine 75 mg/m² subcutaneously Days 1‑7 q28 days (off‑label salvage); stopped promptly due to rapid worsening grade 3 transaminitis driven by hepatic lymphoma infiltration.
# As of 01/04/2026: Awaiting enrolment in a relapse-specific T‑cell lymphoma clinical trial (potential agents include duvelisib, romidepsin, CD3-engaging bispecifics, or CAR-T constructs). No additional systemic anticancer therapy initiated; continuing supportive measures and scheduled repeat PET‑CT in six weeks.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence:
- Ongoing grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy from prior vincristine exposure, contributing to decreased vibration perception in feet; managed with gabapentin.
- Active proximal deep-vein thrombosis (right femoro-popliteal) treated with therapeutic apixaban 5 mg BID; coagulation parameters within target range, no bleeding episodes.
- Grade 3 transaminitis primarily attributable to hepatic lymphoma involvement; bilirubin ≤ 1.2 mg/dL, serum albumin borderline low-normal (~3.5 g/dL), synthetic liver function preserved.
- Resolved historic hepatitis B infection mandates regular HBV DNA monitoring if intensified immune-modulating therapy is considered.
- Performance status limited to ECOG 2, restricting tolerability of aggressive multidrug chemotherapies.
- No clinically evident cardiovascular compromise, renal dysfunction (creatinine 0.9 mg/dL, eGFR ≥ 90 mL/min/1.73 m²), uncontrolled diabetes, or active infections; absent CNS disease or untreated brain involvement.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 55
Sex: Female
Cancer type: Ovarian cancer
Histology: Low-grade serous carcinoma
Current extent: Advanced/metastatic disease with persistent low-volume peritoneal implants (largest ≤1.2 cm) and a solitary 2 cm para-aortic lymph node; disease remains stable on serial imaging while on endocrine therapy (letrozole) as of 18 Oct 2025
Biomarkers: 
- Immunoprofile: CK7+, PAX8+, WT1+, ER ++ strong nuclear, PR + weak, p53 wild-type, Ki‑67 ≈5%
- Tumor DNA NGS: KRAS p.G12D VAF ≈ 45% (pathogenic); NRAS p.Q61K VAF ≈ 8% (likely pathogenic/subclonal); PIK3CA p.E545K VAF ≈ 12% (pathogenic); CCNE1 copy-number gain (~3-fold) amplified; TP53 wild‑type; BRAF wild‑type; FGFR2 fusion non-functional; MSI-stable; TMB low (~3 Mut/Mb); no BRCA1/2 alterations
- Serum tumor marker: CA‑125 moderately elevated at 68 U/mL (stable versus prior measurements)
Treatment history:
# 08/2020: Primary cytoreductive surgery (total hysterectomy‑bilateral salpingo‑oophorectomy, infracolic omentectomy, pelvic/paraaortic lymphadenectomy, diaphragmatic peritonectomy) – R0 resection; FIGO IIIC
# Late 2020–Early 2021: adjuvant carboplatin AUC 5 + paclitaxel 175 mg/m² IV ×4 cycles; tolerability good, brief disease stabilization
# 05/2023–Present: letrozole 2.5 mg oral daily (endocrine maintenance); ongoing with only Grade 1 arthralgia managed with acetaminophen PRN; dose uninterrupted as of 18 Oct 2025
# 09/2023: PET/CT demonstrated new FDG‑avid pelvic sidewall deposits and hypermetabolic para‑aortic node → classified as platinum-refractory; prompted transition to hormonal maintenance
# 07/2024: Cross-sectional CT confirming stable peritoneal implants and unchanged para‑aortic node while on letrozole
# 10/2025: Continued letrozole with stable disease on repeat CT (peritoneal implants ≤1.2 cm, para‑aortic node 1.9 cm) and steady CA‑125; supportive care added vitamin D supplementation for arthritis mitigation
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No evidence of common exclusion criteria: no CNS involvement, adequate hematologic/hepatorenal function, ECOG 0–1, no significant cardiac, pulmonary, infectious, or psychiatric disorders limiting study entry.

No uncontrolled comorbidities – hypertension controlled on lisinopril, hyperlipidaemia medically managed, renal function normal (eGFR ≈ 95 ml/min), hepatic synthetic function preserved, marrow reserves adequate (CBC normal), ECOG 0–1, no active infections, no unresolved CNS disease, no interstitial lung disease, cardiac function uncompromised.

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 70
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Cutaneous malignant melanoma
Histology: Superficial spreading melanoma, ulcerated, Breswell depth 5.2 mm, Clark level IV, high mitotic rate (12/mm²), microsatellitosis present
Current extent: Metastatic disease with progressive multi‑segement hepatic metastases (largest 3.7 cm) and stable pulmonary metastases; no other distant sites identified
Biomarkers: BRAF V600E heterozygous (allele fraction ~38 %); TERT promoter ­124C>T; CDKN2A homozygous deletion; TP53 R175H heterozygous; NF1 truncating alteration; KRAS G12R low‑frequency (~6 %); PIK3CA H1047R (~5 %); STK11 frameshift (~4 %); tumor mutational burden 14 muts/Mb (intermediate); microsatellite stable; PD‑L1 CPS ≈15 %; IHC: S100+, SOX10+, MART‑1+, HMB‑45+, Ki‑67 ≈35 %
Treatment history:
# 2021‑02‑24: Excisonal diagnostic biopsy of left upper‑extremity lesion; margins negative.
# 2023‑06‑xx: Wide local excision of primary melanoma with ≥2 cm radial margins and sentinel lymph node biopsy (2/3 nodes positive with extracapsular extension); pathology showed clear margins.
# 2023‑09‑14 through 2024‑09‑13: Adjuvant pembrolizumab 200 mg IV q3 weeks for twelve months; tolerated with only transient Grade 2 dermatitis.
# 2024‑11‑27 through 2025‑03‑yy: Combined ipilimumab 3 mg/kg + nivolumab 1 mg/kg IV q3 weeks (four‑dose induction); partial regression of pulmonary metastases.
# 2025‑04‑zz: Development of Grade 3 immune‑mediated colitis; ipilimumab ceased, nivolumab continued 240 mg IV q2 weeks.
# 2025‑07‑11 through 2026‑04‑10: Nivolumab maintenance 240 mg IV q2 weeks; pulmonary disease remained partially responsive, hepatic lesions newly appeared and grew, indicating progression.
# 2026‑04‑10: Nivolumab withheld due to confirmed hepatic progression; plan formulated to begin dabrafenib 150 mg orally twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily (start pending approval).
# 2026‑05‑08: Assessed for CD73 inhibitor trial; excluded because of prior Grade 3 gastrointestinal irAE and rapid progression on PD‑1 directed therapy.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: Immune‑related adverse event history includes Grade 3 colitis (high‑dose steroids, now resolved), prior Grade 2 dermatitis (resolved), low‑grade hepatitis (AST/ALT <3×ULN) historically, presently mild transaminitis likely reflecting tumor burden, and Grade 2 fatigue. Chronic hypothyroidism secondary to checkpoint inhibition managed with levothyroxine. Co‑morbidities: controlled hypertension, hyperlipidaemia, pre‑diabetes. No untreated CNS metastases, no active viral hepatitis or HIV, ECOG performance status 1 (fatigued but ambulatory).

cancer_history_summary:
Age: 66
Sex: Male
Cancer type: Invasive lobular carcinoma of the right breast
Histology: Classical invasive lobular carcinoma, grade 2
Current extent: Metastatic – hepatic lesion in segment IV decreased to approx 1.5 × 1.4 cm (stable/regressing); multifocal thoracic and lumbar vertebral osseous metastases remained stable; no new visceral or nodal disease.
Biomarkers:
- Hormone receptors: ER strongly positive (>95%; Allred 8), PR negative (<1%)
- HER2-low (IHC 1+, non-amplified by ISH/FISH)
- Ki‑67 ≈ 30%
- Loss of E-cadherin (CDH1) confirmed; CDH1 p.Glu736Lys (likely pathogenic, VAF 10%)
- PIK3CA p.Glu545Lys (activating hotspot, VAF 7%)
- TP53 p.Tyr220Cys (likely pathogenic, VAF 12%)
- ESR1 p.Asp538Gly (ligand-binding domain mutation, VAF 5%) suggesting AI resistance
- FGFG2 p.Asn549Lys (potential activator, VAF 4%)
- CCND1 amplification (CN 8)
- CDK12 homozygous loss (CN 1)
- PALB2 frameshift (variant of uncertain significance)
- Microsatellite stability (MSI-stable), TMB 3 mut/Mb
- Serum tumor markers: CEA 3.1 ng/mL (stable); CA 15‑3 and CA 27‑29 trends not rechecked since early 2024
Treatment history:
# 12/2020: Diagnostic ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy of right breast confirming invasive lobular carcinoma; initiated tamoxifen 20 mg PO daily.
# 07/2021–12/2021: Weekly paclitaxel 80 → 70 mg/m², total 6 cycles; resulted in partial metabolic response of skeletal disease; incurred Grade 2 peripheral neuropathy (resolved to Grade 1).
# 02/2022–09/2023: Letrozole 2.5 mg PO daily maintenance endocrine therapy combined with zoledronic acid 4 mg IV q12 weeks.
# 08/2023: Biopsy of hepatic lesion reaffirmed ER⁺/PR⁻/HER2‑low phenotype; Ki‑67 ≈ 30%.
# 10/2023: Broad NGS panel revealed listed genetic alterations incl. ESR1 D538G.
# 11/2023–present: Capecitabine 1250 mg/m² PO BID Days 1‑14 of a 21‑day cycle; continued uninterrupted through May 2026 (~>40 cycles); best overall response stable disease with modest decrease of hepatic lesion; manageable toxicities (initial Grade 2 diarrhea resolved, persisting Grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, intermittent mild hand-foot sensation addressed preventively).
# Throughout: Zoledronic acid 4 mg IV q12 weeks for skeletal protection; calcium/Vit D supplementation; routine vaccination updates; referred for germline counseling concerning PALB2 VUS.
general_exclusion_criteria_evidence: No untreated central nervous system metastases; no cardiac insufficiency, pulmonary fibrosis, interstitial pneumonitis, or autoimmune disorders requiring immunosuppression; renal function stable (Cr 0.92 mg/dL), hepatic transaminases <50 U/L, bilirubin ≤1.1 mg/dL; complete blood count within normal ranges; ECOG performance status 1; peripheral neuropathy confined to Grade 1; hypertension and hyperlipidemia well-controlled; HBV, HCV, HIV serologies negative; no active infectious processes.
